titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas W. Delaney to Matthew Nathan, 15 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas F. Delaney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Covering letter sent by Thomas W. Delaney, Crown solicitor for Longford (1862-1939), to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), under-secretary for Ireland. The covering letter relates to a report from a petty sessions case on a separation allowance, paid to the wive's of men fighting in the British army: War Office vs Kiernan. The enclosed report was forwarded to the War Office in London.Sir Matthew Nathan served as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6810/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Longford, April 15 1916
War Office V Keenan
Separation allowance Sir, I beg to enclose copy newspaper
containing report of Petty sessions in
above case I am Sir
Your ObtSrvt . T.W. Delany The Under Secretary Dublin Castle
